relations.subject: Chat flycatcher
relations.evidence: |year=2024 |title=Agricola infuscatus |volume=2024 |article-number=e.T22709040A263876477 |doi=10.2305/IUCN.UK.2024-2.RLTS.T22709040A263876477.en |access-date=12 February 2025}} | genus = Agricola | species = infuscatus | authority = (Smith, A, 1839) | synonyms = Bradornis infuscatusMelaenornis infuscatusSaxicola infuscata }} The chat flycatcher (Agricola infuscatus) is a small passerine bird in the Old World flycatcher family Muscicapidae that is native to southern Africa

relations.subject: Chat flycatcher
relations.evidence: The chat flycatcher is found in South Africa, Botswana and Angola. The flycatcher frequently perches on low-lying bushes and telephone wires. Its natural habitat is dry savanna
